---
title: "Unrefereed Course Notes"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)

note_entries <- c("Title", "Author", "Subject", "Pages", "University", "Contact", "Abstract")

note_data <- c("Alexander-Wallace-Spanier Cohomology", "Hildebrant, John A.", "topology", "20", "Louisiana State University", "deceased", "Topics include Abelian groups; cohomology groups; homomorphisms induced by continuous functions; reduced groups in dimension zero; fully normal spaces; inverse limits and the continuity theorem; strong excision theorems, extension, reduction; the map excision theorem; the Mayer-Vietoris theorem; codimension; marginal, peripheral, and inner points; the map addition theorem; acyclicity; and the Vietoris mapping theorem.")


tibble(note_entries, note_data) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_align(align = "left") |>
  tab_style(
    style= cell_text(v_align = "top"),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) |>
  tab_options( table.width = "80%", column_labels.hidden = TRUE)  


```
- [Download File](./alexander-wallace-spanier-cohomology_hildebrant.pdf)

This course guide was donated to a collection originally gathered by MathNerds. The author retains copyright. You are free to use these materials for your classes.
